answer:
**Cynewulf's *Andreas*, *Elene*, and *Juliana* contain extended narrative miracle scenes, as vivid Old English verse retellings of hagiographic legends.**[^1][^2][^3]

These are the only works in the Falsafa corpus with such scenes. The remainder either use "miracle" metaphorically in poetry/philosophy (e.g., Iqbal's *Bang-e-Dara* parts or Ghalib's *Diwan*), reference miracles abstractly (e.g., Comte/Dunoyer's legal/theoretical treatises), or lack narrative elements entirely (e.g., Smṛti legal codes). No Indic Kawi/Sanskrit works or French/German theory texts feature miracle narratives.

- ***Andreas*** narrates Apostle Andrew's missionary journey to cannibalistic Mermedonia: God sends angels as disguised sailors; Andrew miraculously breaks Matthew's chains and frees 192 captives unseen; he survives floggings (body heals instantly, blood turns to flowers); causes a city-wide flood from a pillar that swallows enemies; raises drowned youths.[^4]
- ***Elene*** recounts Helena (Constantine's mother) finding the True Cross: Constantine's visionary cross-sign wins a battle; Judas locates/baptizes via smoke miracle; dead youth revives on the True Cross; nails emerge in divine fire; Helena adorns the Cross, which heals perpetually.[^5]
- ***Juliana*** depicts the saint's martyrdom: she binds/interrogates a demon-angel; survives whipping, stretching, boiling lead/fire (kills 75 torturers instead); her corpse procession inspires conversions.[^6]

No other corpus works (37 total) match this; searches for "miracle(s)", "healed", "true cross", etc., confirm.

[^1]: Cynewulf, *Andreas*, [Chapter 1](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/).
[^2]: Cynewulf, *Elene*, [Chapter 1](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/).
[^3]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [Chapter 1](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/).
[^4]: *Andreas*, Ch. 1, ¶s. [p-da0c5e](.../01/translation/#p-da0c5e) (chains break), [p-997c80](.../01/translation/#p-997c80) (body heals), [p-2e50c1](.../01/translation/#p-2e50c1) (flood), [p-0609a7](.../01/translation/#p-0609a7) (resurrection).
[^5]: *Elene*, Ch. 1, ¶s. [p-acdc07](.../01/translation/#p-acdc07) (vision), [p-60c0a6](.../01/translation/#p-60c0a6) (smoke/resurrection), [p-306e06](.../01/translation/#p-306e06) (nails).
[^6]: *Juliana*, Ch. 1, ¶s. [p-a370d7](.../01/translation/#p-a370d7) (demon bound), [p-3a01e6](.../01/translation/#p-3a01e6) (fire/lead unharmed).
